artifact_archival_class_identifiers.summary: Canonical release identity anchors and governed release-facing identifiers.
Release manifests, current release indices, and retained historical release-index snapshots.
Release contract profile, semantic contract pins, governance anchors, and related compatibility-envelope artifacts.
Bundle roots, payload artifacts, profiles, packs, locks, and related shipped release payloads.
Changelog layers, operator transaction records, and deprecation or yank or supersession history.
Offline archive bundles, archive records, retained history trees, and explicit recovery baselines.
Planning checkpoints and support bundles kept distinct from release canon.
Scratch, cache, staging, and temporary local outputs without release archival duty.

mirror_class_identifiers.summary: Primary availability surface for governed release distribution.
Secondary availability surface for redundancy without canonical authorship.
Channel-scoped or policy-filtered availability surface exposing only an allowed subset.
Mirror or seed surface optimized for offline survivability and reconstruction rather than routine distribution.
Derived reference surface that exposes summaries or selected support artifacts without becoming release canon.
Artifact class intentionally excluded from release mirroring.

archive_duty_vs_mirror_duty_distinctions: Archive duty preserves exact historical recoverability and identity continuity.
Mirror duty provides policy-governed availability or discoverability through derived surfaces.
Archive presence does not imply preference, latest status, or universal visibility.
Mirror visibility does not imply canonical authorship or complete historical retention.

retention_survivability_categories.summary: Exact retained history required for reconstructability and audit.
Availability duty for current or policy-governed release exposure.
Retention or mirroring class supporting extinction prevention and offline reconstruction.
Retention posture for planning and support bundles that remain distinct from release canon.
No archival or mirroring duty by default.

yanked_deprecated_superseded_archival_co: Yanked artifacts may become non-preferred or filtered from mirrors without ceasing to exist historically.
Deprecated artifacts remain visible or recoverable according to explicit policy and are not silently erased.
Superseded artifacts retain exact archival continuity even when successors become preferred in mirrors or selection policy.

canonical_surfaces: canonical release index and retained historical release-index snapshots
operator transaction records where later doctrine explicitly defines them
canonical_vs_derived_distinctions.rule: Archive copies preserve artifacts and mirrors expose them, but neither storage layout nor derived carrier surfaces create canonical release truth.

ambiguities_eliminated: mirror does not mean canonical source
archive does not mean preferred or latest
yanked does not mean historically erased
checkpoint bundles are not release canon
downstream_refinement_prompts: future zeta continuity and disaster-recovery planning
